metadata.archetype_justification: Zipkin is primarily a read-heavy data access layer — its core function is ingesting trace spans (via collectors) and serving read queries over stored trace data (via the Query API). Database queries dominate the logic. Classified as data-gateway.

top_gaps.title: Network Security
top_gaps.gap: No IaC defining VPC, subnets, or security groups
top_gaps.impact: No network segmentation or isolation defined

top_gaps.title: API Entry Point
top_gaps.gap: No API Gateway, ALB, or managed entry point defined
top_gaps.impact: Direct service exposure without throttling or auth

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code Coverage
top_gaps.gap: No Terraform, CloudFormation, or CDK found — 0% IaC coverage

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or audit logging configuration

top_gaps.title: Encryption at Rest
top_gaps.gap: No KMS or encryption configuration for data stores
top_gaps.impact: Data at rest is not encrypted via managed keys

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (INF-Q11 >= 2). GitHub Actions workflows present for test, deploy, and release.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that triggers deployments, checks build status, and manages releases via the existing GitHub Actions pipeline surface.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Formalize API access to the CI/CD pipeline (GitHub API tokens with appropriate scopes for workflow dispatch).

quick_agent_wins.name: Observability Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Structured logging and tracing in place (OPS-Q1 = 4). Zipkin IS a tracing system with full distributed tracing support.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that queries traces, correlates spans with service behavior, and suggests root causes for latency issues.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Expose Zipkin's own query API as an agent tool interface with appropriate authentication.

decomposition_strategy.rationale: For a data-gateway archetype, full microservices decomposition is not appropriate. The bounded decomposition (separating write and read paths) provides independent scaling where it matters most. Start by containerizing to EKS as-is, then selectively separate the collector (write) path from the query (read) path.

approaches.name: Strangler Fig (Parallel Track)
approaches.applicability: Applicable — extract collectors as independent services while keeping query API in the monolith initially.

approaches.name: Conditional / Adaptive
approaches.applicability: Best fit — containerize as-is on EKS, then selectively extract high-value components (collectors for write-path scaling).

approaches.name: Big-Bang Rewrite
approaches.applicability: Not recommended — the existing codebase is well-structured with clean interfaces.

effort_factors.module_boundaries: Clear Maven multi-module structure with clean interfaces (Low effort signal)
effort_factors.data_coupling: Shared StorageComponent abstraction but single database per deployment (Medium effort signal)
effort_factors.stored_procedures: None — all logic in application layer (Low effort signal)
effort_factors.communication_patterns: Async collectors already supported via Kafka/RabbitMQ (Low effort signal)
effort_factors.cicd_maturity: Application CI exists but no IaC deployment (Medium effort signal)
effort_factors.test_coverage: Comprehensive integration tests exist (Low effort signal)

findings.title: No Network Security Infrastructure
findings.description: No IaC defines VPC, subnets, security groups, NACLs, or any network segmentation. Docker Compose examples expose services on default Docker networking with no isolation.
findings.gap: Zero network security infrastructure defined. Services would be deployed without isolation.
findings.recommendation: Define VPC with private subnets for databases and public subnet for the Zipkin server. Create security groups with least-privilege rules. Use VPC endpoints for AWS service access.

findings.title: No Managed API Entry Point
findings.description: No API Gateway, ALB, CloudFront, or any managed entry point is defined. The Zipkin server exposes port 9411 directly without throttling or authentication.
findings.gap: Direct service exposure with no throttling, authentication, or request validation at the edge.
findings.recommendation: Deploy an ALB in front of EKS, or use API Gateway for the query API with throttling and authentication.

findings.title: No Auto-Scaling Configuration
findings.description: No auto-scaling configuration exists. No ASG, ECS service scaling, EKS HPA, or Lambda concurrency limits are defined.
findings.gap: All capacity is statically provisioned with no mechanism to handle traffic spikes.
findings.recommendation: Configure HPA on EKS based on CPU/memory and custom metrics. Consider Karpenter for node-level autoscaling.

findings.title: No Backup and Recovery Configuration
findings.description: No backup or recovery configuration exists. No backup_retention_period, no aws_backup_plan, no PITR configuration.
findings.gap: No backup strategy for any data store. Data loss risk in case of failure.
findings.recommendation: Enable Aurora automated backups with PITR, OpenSearch automated snapshots, and define an AWS Backup plan.

findings.title: No High Availability Configuration
findings.description: No multi-AZ deployment configuration exists. Docker Compose examples run single instances of each service.
findings.gap: Single-instance deployment with no fault isolation. An instance failure takes down the entire service.
findings.recommendation: Deploy on EKS across 2+ AZs with multiple replicas. Configure Aurora Multi-AZ and multi-AZ OpenSearch domain.

findings.title: Zero Infrastructure as Code Coverage
findings.description: No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm charts, or Kustomize files exist. All infrastructure would need to be created manually.
findings.gap: 0% IaC coverage. All infrastructure changes are manual and non-reproducible.
findings.recommendation: Create Terraform modules defining EKS cluster, VPC/networking, Aurora MySQL, OpenSearch domain, MSK cluster, ALB, and monitoring.

findings.title: Partial CI/CD Automation
findings.description: GitHub Actions provides automated build, test, and Docker image publication. However, no infrastructure deployment automation exists — no Terraform plan/apply, no CDK deploy in the pipeline.
findings.gap: Application CI/CD exists but infrastructure deployment is entirely manual.
findings.recommendation: Extend GitHub Actions with Terraform plan/apply stages and Helm deployment to EKS.

findings.title: Containerized but No Managed Orchestration
findings.description: Multi-stage Dockerfile exists producing Alpine-based JRE images. Docker Compose files demonstrate deployment. No IaC defines ECS/EKS/Fargate resources.
findings.gap: Containers exist but are not deployed to managed container orchestration via infrastructure code.
findings.recommendation: Deploy on Amazon EKS with a Helm chart. Define EKS cluster infrastructure in Terraform/CDK.

findings.title: Databases Self-Managed in Docker
findings.description: MySQL, Cassandra, and Elasticsearch all deployed as self-managed Docker containers. No managed database services defined in IaC.
findings.gap: All databases are self-managed with manual patching, backup, and scaling responsibilities.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Aurora MySQL, Amazon Keyspaces or Cassandra on EKS, and Amazon OpenSearch Service.

findings.title: No Audit Logging
findings.description: No CloudTrail or equivalent audit logging configuration exists. No forensic trail for API access or infrastructure changes.
findings.gap: No CloudTrail or audit logging infrastructure defined.
findings.recommendation: Define CloudTrail in IaC with log file validation and S3 Object Lock for immutable storage.

findings.title: No Encryption at Rest
findings.description: No KMS or encryption at rest configuration exists. Docker-based databases use default unencrypted storage.
findings.gap: No encryption at rest configured for any data store.
findings.recommendation: Enable Aurora storage encryption with KMS, OpenSearch domain encryption, and define customer-managed KMS keys.

findings.title: No API Authentication
findings.description: No API authentication configured. Query API and collector endpoints are open by default with CORS allowed-origins: '*'.
findings.gap: All API endpoints are open with no authentication.
findings.recommendation: Deploy API Gateway with OAuth2/JWT authentication. Implement API key auth or mTLS for collector endpoints.

findings.title: No Centralized Identity Integration
findings.description: No centralized identity provider integration. The application manages no authentication at all.
findings.gap: No identity provider integration — open access to all endpoints.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Amazon Cognito for user authentication. Use IAM roles for service-to-service auth.

findings.title: Single Deployable Monolith
findings.description: All collectors, storage backends, query API, and UI packaged into one Spring Boot application. Well-structured Maven multi-module layout with clean interfaces.
findings.gap: Monolith with identifiable modules but shared deployment lifecycle. Components cannot scale independently.
findings.recommendation: Consider separating collectors (write path) from query API (read path) as independent deployments. This is bounded decomposition appropriate for a data-gateway.

findings.title: No Formal API Versioning Strategy
findings.description: No OpenAPI spec exists. URL path versioning (/api/v2/) is used in code but no formal strategy or backward compatibility guarantees documented.
findings.gap: Ad hoc versioning with no formal API specification or compatibility guarantees.
findings.recommendation: Generate OpenAPI specification from Java API. Document versioning strategy and backward compatibility guarantees.

findings.title: Environment Variable Endpoints Without Dynamic Discovery
findings.description: Service endpoints configured via environment variables (CASSANDRA_CONTACT_POINTS, ES_HOSTS, MYSQL_HOST). Eureka support exists but is opt-in and legacy.
findings.gap: No dynamic service discovery by default. Endpoint changes require redeployment.
findings.recommendation: On EKS, leverage Kubernetes-native service discovery (CoreDNS). Consider AWS Cloud Map for cross-cluster discovery.

findings.title: No Object Storage for Data
findings.description: Trace data stored only in databases (Cassandra, Elasticsearch, MySQL). No S3 or object storage tier for archival.
findings.gap: No object storage tier for long-term data archival or cost-effective retention.
findings.recommendation: Consider S3 for long-term trace archival with lifecycle policies to reduce database storage costs.

findings.title: Database Versions Not Pinned in Infrastructure
findings.description: Versions defined only in Docker image tags with no explicit version pinning in IaC. Driver versions pinned in POM but engine versions undocumented.
findings.gap: No explicit engine version pinning in infrastructure code. EOL status undocumented.
findings.recommendation: When defining databases in IaC, explicitly pin engine versions. Document supported versions and create an update procedure.

findings.title: No SLO Definitions
findings.description: No SLO definitions exist. No CloudWatch alarms on latency percentiles or error budgets. Micrometer metrics available but not consumed by SLO definitions.
findings.gap: No formal SLOs for query latency, ingestion rate, or availability.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs for query API p99 latency, span ingestion success rate, and service availability. Implement error budget tracking.

findings.title: No Business Metrics
findings.description: Prometheus metrics via Micrometer exist but appear to be infrastructure metrics only. No custom business metrics defined.
findings.gap: No business outcome metrics (trace volume, service coverage, query patterns).
findings.recommendation: Publish custom CloudWatch metrics for traces ingested per minute, unique services, query volume, and storage growth.

findings.title: No Alerting Configuration
findings.description: No alerting exists. No CloudWatch alarms, anomaly detection, or integration with incident management tools.
findings.gap: No alerting. Degradation or outages would not be detected automatically.
findings.recommendation: Define CloudWatch alarms for error rate spikes, latency anomalies, and storage throttle activation. Enable anomaly detection.

findings.title: No Staged Deployment Strategy
findings.description: Docker images built and pushed to registries. No canary, blue/green, or rolling deployment with traffic shifting defined.
findings.gap: No staged rollout. Deployments go directly to production with no safety net.
findings.recommendation: Implement canary deployments on EKS using Argo Rollouts with automated analysis on error rate and latency.

findings.title: No Incident Response Automation
findings.description: No runbooks, no automated incident response, no self-healing. All incident response is ad hoc.
findings.gap: No runbooks or automation for common failure scenarios.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for common failures. Automate pod restart on health failures and collector scaling on queue depth.

findings.title: No Observability Ownership
findings.description: No CODEOWNERS for observability, no dashboards with owners, no SLO team attribution. Open-source community governance.
findings.gap: No observability ownership model for organizational deployment.
findings.recommendation: Define dashboards per component, assign alarm owners, and document escalation paths.

findings.title: No Resource Tagging
findings.description: No resource tagging because no IaC exists. No tagging standards documented.
findings.gap: No tagging strategy for cost allocation, ownership, or environment identification.
findings.recommendation: Implement tagging standard (Environment, Service, Team, CostCenter) with Terraform default_tags and Config enforcement.

findings.title: Unified Data Access Layer — Minor Gaps
findings.description: Well-defined StorageComponent abstraction with implementations for each backend. Some auxiliary operations (dependency linker, autocomplete) have backend-specific paths outside the main abstraction.
findings.gap: Mostly centralized data access with some auxiliary code paths bypassing the unified interface.
findings.recommendation: No immediate action needed. The existing storage abstraction is well-designed. Consider consolidating auxiliary operations through the same interface over time.

findings.title: Minimal MySQL-Specific SQL Constructs
findings.description: MySQL schema uses ENGINE=InnoDB ROW_FORMAT=COMPRESSED which are MySQL-specific but compatible with Aurora MySQL. No stored procedures, triggers, or functions. JOOQ provides SQL abstraction.
findings.gap: Minimal MySQL-specific DDL (ROW_FORMAT=COMPRESSED, ENGINE=InnoDB) that is non-portable to non-MySQL engines but compatible with Aurora MySQL.
findings.recommendation: No action needed for Aurora MySQL migration. If migrating to PostgreSQL in the future, the ROW_FORMAT and ENGINE clauses would need removal.

findings.title: Secrets via Environment Variables Without Rotation
findings.description: Production credentials managed via environment variables with no plaintext in source. GitHub Actions uses GitHub Secrets. Elasticsearch supports dynamic credential refresh. However, no dedicated secrets management service or rotation is configured.
findings.gap: Environment variables for secrets without rotation. No Secrets Manager or Vault integration.
findings.recommendation: Integrate AWS Secrets Manager for database credentials with automated rotation. Use External Secrets Operator on EKS to sync secrets.

findings.title: Vulnerability Scanning Without Registry Scanning
findings.description: Trivy runs in CI scanning for HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities and secrets. Docker image uses minimal Alpine-based JRE and runs as non-root. However, no container registry scanning (ECR) or formal patch management process exists.
findings.gap: Vulnerability scanning in CI but no container image scanning in a registry. No formal patch management process.
findings.recommendation: Push images to ECR and enable ECR image scanning. Consider AWS Inspector for continuous vulnerability monitoring of running containers on EKS.

findings.title: Integration Tests Without End-to-End Deployment Tests
findings.description: Comprehensive Docker-based integration tests via Testcontainers covering each storage backend and collector. Tests run in CI. However, no end-to-end deployment smoke tests verify the full deployed stack.
findings.gap: Integration tests cover individual backends in isolation, not the full deployment topology. No post-deployment smoke tests.
findings.recommendation: Add post-deployment smoke tests that verify the full stack (collector → storage → query API) works end-to-end in a staging environment.

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2 = 2 (primary trigger < 3) AND INF-Q1 = 2 (supporting trigger < 3). Both conditions met.

detail.current_state: Single deployable monolith (zipkin-server) packaging all collectors, storage backends, query API, and UI. Well-structured Maven multi-module layout with clear interfaces but shared deployment lifecycle.
immediate_actions: Separate collectors (write path) from query API (read path) as independent deployments
Define service boundaries using the existing Maven module structure
Deploy on EKS with independent scaling for read vs write paths

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1 = 2 (threshold met) but container definitions found extensively (29 Docker files, multi-stage Dockerfile). Contextual guard: SHALL NOT trigger if container definitions exist.

triggering_questions.note: Score meets threshold but contextual guard blocks: 29 Docker files and Dockerfiles exist

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Container definitions found extensively in the repository (main Dockerfile, 12 test image Dockerfiles, 14 docker-compose files). The workload is already containerized — what's missing is managed orchestration (EKS/ECS), not containerization itself.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4 = 3 (does not meet threshold < 3). No commercial DB engines detected — MySQL (via MariaDB client), Cassandra, Elasticsearch are all open-source.

triggering_questions.note: Score does not meet < 3 threshold. No stored procedures or proprietary SQL.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2 = 2 (self-managed databases in Docker), DATA-Q3 = 2 (versions unpinned in IaC)

detail.current_state: MySQL, Cassandra, and Elasticsearch self-managed in Docker containers with no managed database infrastructure

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4 = 4 for data-gateway archetype (sync reads are correct design). No data processing workloads requiring managed analytics detected.

triggering_questions.note: Score does not meet < 3 threshold. Archetype-calibrated: sync is correct for data-gateway.

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No data processing, ETL, or analytics workloads detected. The messaging collectors (Kafka, RabbitMQ) are for span ingestion, not data analytics. INF-Q4 scores 4 due to archetype calibration.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10 = 1 (no IaC), INF-Q11 = 2 (partial CI/CD), OPS-Q5 = 2 (no canary/blue-green)

detail.current_state: Zero IaC, partial CI/CD (application only), no deployment strategy, good integration tests
immediate_actions: Create Helm chart for EKS deployment

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context is 'Distributed tracing system' with no AI-related signal terms.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context.
